entry.title: Re: Curricula Completion
entry.content: @syderny, as it stands, it wouldn't be possible to manually complete a curricula. The curricula completion would be granted once its courses/requirements are concluded. It'd be necessary to relate the courses and the curricula and the users to have this scenario achieved.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic approval process for Learning
entry.content: @Janine_Zeh2023, the approval role concept could be reviewed to understand if that'd fulfill your scenario.

entry.title: Send Different Activation Emails to Newly Created Users Based on Their Account Attributes
entry.content:
When you hire new employees, one of your first tasks is to create accounts for them. But is it possible for the different users to get different activation emails when the accounts are created in SAP Cloud Identity services via an automated user provisioning process? 

The answer is yes - you can configure different email template sets for account activation of newly created users based on user specific values of the attributes when Cloud Identity Services acts as an authenticating identity provider. 

 

But how exactly to do this? 

Well, this is what we are trying to explain in this blog. As an example scenario we will use the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding scenario where the users are replicated from a SuccessFactors system to SAP Cloud Identity services by the Identity Provisioning service. In a nutshell: "When a new hire is created in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding, they’re provisioned in Identity Authentication and the provisioned users receive an activation email that they can use to set the password and access their SAP SuccessFactors application." (Setting up SAP Identity Authentication Service for New Hires Using System for Cross-domain Identity ...) 

However, we don’t aim to cover the end-to-end onboarding scenario, just the customization of the activation emails. Let’s assume that we have the following three divisions in the company: EMEA, APJ, NA and the onboarding users for each of them should receive a different account activation email. 

So, in the SCIM API of SAP SuccessFactors these values are returned in the “division” attribute (part of the SCIM Enterprise schema) of the onboarding users.

Let's start with the configuration steps.

Prerequisites

You must be an administrator in SAP Cloud Identity Services with the Manage Tenant Configuration role enabled. 

Create and Configure Custom Email Template Sets in Cloud Identity Services

1. Access the administration console->Application & Resources->Email Templates.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

2. Create custom email templates for the different divisions. 
Choose the Add button, name the set, and save. Repeat this for every division providing the respective names for them - EMEA, APJ, NA.











 

 

As a result, the new template sets appear in the list on the left:



 

 

 

 

3. Create template files in plain text and HTM. 
   a. Choose the On-Behalf Registration tab and select Default template for that set from the list on the left. Initially only the English version is available. 
   b. Open the plain text version, copy and paste it in a new file, edit the text, name it, and save it as a .txt file (repeat this step for every division)
   c. Open the HTML version, copy and paste it in a new file, edit the text, name it and save it as a .html file (repeat this step for every division)

 

 

 

 

4. Choose the On-Behalf Registration tab -> select the EMEA template for that set from the list on the left -> choose Add Language  and fill in the fields in the pop-up -> Save. For the files filed use the .txt and .html files that you created in Step 3.









 

Once created, every template set receives a unique ID. When the template set is selected in the list the ID is visible in the URL of the administration console and follows the pattern: https://<sap-identity-services-host>/admin/#/emailTemplateSets/<email-template-set-id>/…
Thus, we can extract the ID value after “emailTemplateSets/” till the next “/”






In our specific scenario, the IDs are:
EMEA - d490de93-f633-42b7-b31f-78bf12efedea
APJ - de464cea-023a-4f51-b9dc-77eb435c034a
NA - bf9d92c6-89c2-4305-81f0-4f5c4bb9216b 

 

Configure the Target System

1. Access the administration console->Identity Provisioning->Target Systems

2. Find the target system that corresponds to the Cloud Identity Services tenant (part of the user provisioning flow for onboarding users from SAP SuccessFactors). See Configure Transformations in SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning 

3. Go to Transformation tab, switch to JSON editor.

4. Choose Edit.

4. Find the following fragment:

 

 

 

 

       {

          "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",

          "constant": "%ias.onboardee.email.template.id.attribute%",

          "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",

          "scope": "createEntity"

        },

 

 

 

 

and substitute it with the following one:

 

 

 

 

 {

          "condition": "($.userType == 'ONBOARDEE') && ($['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['division'] == 'EMEA')",

          "constant": "d490de93-f633-42b7-b31f-78bf12efedea",
          "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",

          "scope": "createEntity"

        },

        {

          "condition": "($.userType == 'ONBOARDEE') && ($['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['division'] == 'APJ')",

          "constant": "de464cea-023a-4f51-b9dc-77eb435c034a",

          "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",

          "scope": "createEntity"

        },

        {

          "condition": "($.userType == 'ONBOARDEE') && ($['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['division'] == 'NA')",

          "constant": "bf9d92c6-89c2-4305-81f0-4f5c4bb9216b",

          "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",

          "scope": "createEntity"

        },

 

 

 

 

6. Add a description, save and confirm the transformation changes.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Result  

When the new employee is created in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding, and provisioned in Cloud Identity Services, the provisioned users receive different activation emails depending on the division they are part of. Following the link in the activation mail they can use to set the password and access their SAP SuccessFactors application.

entry.title: Re: SAP SF Learning - If someone is marked No Show could...
entry.content: I see. Maybe removing the "for credit" tokens from the "ApmSupervisorLearningPlanNotification" template. However, this could lead to blank notifications being received by the manager (when completions take place).

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps now returns attributes with process instances
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski ,

Thank you for the great tutorial! I really appreciate how seamlessly build process automation integrates with build apps—definitely a big step toward a more consistent platform.

While adapting your process, I had a question: Is it possible to update attributes from build apps? For example, in your case, the manager should not only be able to approve the task in My Inbox but also directly in build apps, including adding fields like comments there.

Benefit could be that also other user - not responsible for the tasks itself, but inrested in the status - could see it and enter comments, like "Whats the current status" (saved for example in a sepertae database or in build process automation).

So far, it seems only the status can be updated—is that correct? Or could that be possible by calling a API (like the old way)?

I’m new to this community platform, so I hope this is the right place to ask questions.

All the best from Germany,
Heiko

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content: I am interested

entry.title: How to pass the Order Quantity Value in Migration Template for SD-Sales Contract?
entry.content:
Hi All,

The Order Quantity field(KWMENG) is not available in Standard template in SD-Sales Contract SAP Migration Object. But when we are passing the data, it is giving error: Please enter order quantity in quantity field of the same name V5(MsgClass) 046(MsgNo.).

When i opened the error details there is procedure to pass the order quantity: To create items relevant for delivery in service contracts, you use the change to double spaced entry option. There you can enter your data in the 'Order quantity' field.

What is "double spaced entry option" in the xml template? How to use that functionality.

Please suggest the solution.

entry.title: Re: Dashboard Design - Science & Standards, Not Random Charts & Art. (Part One)
entry.content: @Daniel +1. I also really like to bring in 'context' and all the IBCS standards! Only I still find it very unfortunate to face (many) practical product limitations when trying to apply them very rigorously in real-life SAC projects. For example: variance charts do not support grouped items (Rank Others/remaining) which is also not supported on live models, amongst many other things, ...

entry.content:
Dear Experts.

how can I cancel a running backup operation in MaxDB ?

I did not find anything else but the backup_cancel command which doesn't work 😕 Thanks a lot for any hints!

entry.title: Re: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!

entry.title: Re: Learning License User Types in SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Hello kchamarthy please clarify as I think there is a contradiction in this text:

In one part it states Functional users don't have access to the system:

"Who is a Functional Learning User?

Employees and non-employees whose learning history records are recorded within Learning but are not given access to the Learning management system are classified as FUNCTIONAL"

While in the other paragraph it says functional users can launch a course:

Functional user count includes the below criteria :

All the users who have ACTIVE status and are classified as Functional user and not using Learning market place in the LMS

All users who have one learning history recorded in the Learning system for an external or internal course or have a course enrolled or launched a course on their learning plan

I'd like to understand whether functional users (in case of our company these would be contractors) can access SF learning to complete compliance courses.

Thank you

Martyna

entry.title: Re: Integration between Cloud ALM and non-SAP architectur...
entry.content: Thanks John, that's clear for now.

entry.title: Re: batch input stops on warning message Ki166 - for true...
entry.content: You can change the systeme behavior with OBA5, for Message area KI, message 166, put it as W 'Warning' in dialog and S 'Note in footer' for Batch Input. But insure that the system will provide correct data.

entry.title: Re: Consume local OData Service within same backend system
entry.content:
Hi @Tisha_Haria 

I'm learning about Clean Core, but it is still confusing. Do you have some link that further explains this specific piece "As a part of clean core guidelines, it is a best practice to used SAP released APIs to fetch the data within S4HANA system instead of directly querying on unreleased CDS views. "

I mean, I just would like to confirm that SAP suggest to use Local APIs instead of unreleased CDS Views.

Regards.

entry.title: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I need to solve a problem related to billing prints. The client has two types of forms in the system: Form A for several countries and Form B, specific only for Poland. The problem concerns a certain type of invoice used by all sales organizations. In the billing type configuration, Form A is indicated, and in order for Poland to print its billing using Form B, I need to update the configuration in NACE. The question is whether I can limit the visibility of Form B on the billing printout list only to the Polish sales department in the configuration? 

I am attaching a screenshot below to better illustrate the example:

Is it possible to solve this issue by confguration so that the Polish sales organization can see both Form A and B, while the other sales organizations can only see Form A?

 

Thanks in advance experts!

entry.title: Re: Dashboard Design - Science & Standards, Not Random Charts & Art. (Part One)
entry.content:
@JefB I would point you to the SAP Customer Influence website where you can add your own feature requests and these will be looked at / considered by Product Management. Point your browser here and log your requests. Can't promise it will be fixed / added in the next release but PM do look / use these entries.

D

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adaptation Transport Organizer
entry.content: @Jocelyn_Dart It is a bit confusing about what to use in the private cloud to move extensions. According to the following learning material (https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/get-started-with-in-app-extensibility-in-sap-s-4hana/transporting-extensions_a2719341-2280-4c2a-a4b4-a5691820d52c) we are supposed to use export/import of software collection in SAP S4Hana Cloud (that one can assume includes public and private) and ATO for on-premise (explicitly mentioned without reference to the private cloud). It seems though that ATO is used for the private cloud and on-premise, whereas the export/import is used in the public cloud. Is that right?

entry.title: Re: Unable to logon to ASE with Interactive SQL using SSL
entry.content:
Hello Ryan and thank you ! You were right about the ssl client certifcate ($DSQUERY.txt) designated for the import to the java keystore. It not only contained the client certificate that I created but also other certificates that my ASE uses to authenticate users via AD/LDAP. And indeed, that client certificate was appended to the bottom of the file (obviously). I extracted it into a separate file and imported only this file to the keystore. No I am able to logon with SSL through Interactive SQL 🙂

I was able to check which certificate in the chain was the client certificate by running this command on the $DSQUERY.txt file

 

f=$SYBASE/$SYBASE_ASE/certificates/$DSQUERY.txt
i=`grep -c 'BEGIN CERTIFICATE' $f`
while [ $i -gt 0 ]; do
awk "BEGIN{nr=$i;i=0}"'/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/{nr--;i=1}nr==0&&i==1{print $0}/END CERTIFICATE/{i=0}' $f | \
$SAP_JRE8/bin/keytool -printcert | head -n 10
i=$((i - 1)) # Correct way to decrement in ksh
done

 

I sourced this command from the SSL troubleshooting note 3152010  - https://me.sap.com/notes/3152010/E

Note: you may need to replace the 'let i--' with '$((i - 1))' for it to work

entry.title: Re: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)
entry.content: A normal access sequence that contains the sales organization is not sufficient?

entry.title: Re: BRS (Bank Reconciliation Statement)
entry.content:
Hi @sai_kumar ,

F002 is a two-step posting rule. The first posting is done from main bank account to the tech account (+++++++090). This posting seems to be ok as your balance on 11000100 was updated correctly.

The second posting area was not done automatically. According to the posting rule it should be posted from the tech account to the checkrec account (+++++++060) This is the reason the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app proposes this account.

 

Best regards, Joerg Rolapp

entry.title: When will Standard API - API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV be Deleted
entry.content:
We are using API - API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV.

We are aware this will be discontinued in the future. Is there a timeline on when exactly this API will be discontinued?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: When will Standard API - API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SR...
entry.content: The API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV API will work until 2508, this date is subject to changes.

entry.title: Re: Why we need Women in Tech
entry.content:
Hi, 

Was hired straight out of college to go and work for SAP in Germany in 1989.

Since then I have lived and worked in 10 countries (Australia, Bermuda, Belgium, France, Finland, Germany, Ireland, New Zealand, Switzerland, USA). 

Women can be Techie: We can be Analysts, Configurers, Contract Negotiators, Landscape Architects, Integration Leads, Project Managers, Test Leads. 

We can be ANYTHING !!!     

Be Bold. Be Strong. If you have a great idea.... talk about it! (but make sure YOU get the credit). 

Train and learn: adopt new skills, new approaches, new techniques. Do not be shy. 

Always learn, adapt and grow.  

We can, and we will, Succeed !!

entry.title: Re: Transfer of Service Data for Customers entitled to several SAP Cloud ALM Tenants
entry.content:

This is in reference to: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-alm-tenant-extension/bc-p/13853926/highlight/true

As there are a few customer who are yet to provision their CALM tenant, how do we segregate the data(service) on customer number level ? Can that be done still ?

entry.content:
Hello,

Now the Invoice pricing and services date are copied from delivery. I need that these date are equal to sy-datum. In the "Copying Requirements" (VTFL) is inserted the routine 004. How I can change that logic?

Thanks and regards.

Antonio

entry.title: Re: SAP BI 4.3: What’s New - Automation Framework renamed as Workflow Assistant
entry.content: We are looking to change the webi data source from UNV to UNX. Tried to enable and start the Workflow assistant but its not working. We are on Linux, BI 4.3 SP2. When I start the Workflow assistant nothing happens, there is no Successfully started message in the log file. Please advise if anyone had run into these issues. Other than this there is no other SAP BO application where you can automate the data source change for webi reports. If SAP is going to stop UNV they better provide a tool to convert thee reports. Thank you!

entry.title: SSO Multiple Emarsys accounts against single SAP IAS Tenant
entry.content:
Hi experts.

We are in a situation where we need some help configuring SSO for two Emarsys accounts, against a single IAS Tenant (our production IAS Tenant).

We have two accounts, say EMARSYS-01 and EMARSYS-02

We manually created the application for EMARSYS-01 in our IAS tenant, following this tutorial Single Sign-On:: How to enable Single Sign-On with SAP Identity Authentication Service – Emarsys

The first acoount worked like a charm at first try (from IAS side and from Emarsys side), so we went for the second account, EMARSYS-02.

Again, following the previous oficial tutorial we found that we can't duplicate the entity ID, for this tenant.

So we tried to put something like "suite-sso-emarsys-02"  in the "name" field while creating the second application.

When we tried to configure SSO, it give us the common error "Error: Identity Provider could not process the authentication request received. Delete your browser cache and stored cookies, and restart your browser. If you still experience issues after doing this, please contact your administrator."

Fiddling in the communication, we've found that Emarsys is sending "suite-sso" as issuer. That's is the reason for the previous error.

<ns1:Issuer Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:entity">suite-sso </ns1:Issuer>

We can't find where to change this ID on Emarsys, and we need to have both accounts on SAP IAS (or even more if we keep working with the solution).

Additionally, I can't find an efficient way to propagate it during login. How can I do this?

entry.title: Re: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)

It seems to me that it's a matter of completing the conditions in the configuration for Access Sequences, but I'm not entirely sure how to do it correctly.

entry.title: Re: How to filter a bar graph by dimensions from the drop...
entry.content:
In which case you need to populate a dropdown with the dimensions, then set the dropdown to switch dimensions in/out of the chart
(With additional code enhancement you can only add specific dimensions to the dropdown)

Populate the dropdown with all dimensions in the underlying model:
var dimlist=Chart_1.getDataSource().getDimensions();
for (var x=0;x<dimlist.length;x++) {
Dropdown_1.addItem(dimlist[x].id,dimlist[x].description);}

Dropdown code to swap the dimension in the chart:

var Dim=Chart_1.getDimensions(Feed.CategoryAxis)[0];
var Key=this.getSelectedKey();
Chart_1.removeDimension(Dim,Feed.CategoryAxis);
Chart_1.addDimension(Key,Feed.CategoryAxis);

entry.title: Re: create a blog button is disabled. Could anyone please...
entry.content: Hi @BinduSri - please send an email to community@sap.com so that we can help troubleshoot.

entry.title: Re: My app can not export PDF anymore after Windows 11 up...
entry.content:
Sorry it's been so long since I've used VS 6 and CR XI I don't recall what the export dll is.

Current versions export dll's are crxf_*.dll, it could be the same for XI, I don't recall now what it used...

In CR Designer open the report and if you click on Help.. About... More Info it lists the dll's used, export the report and then look for something with PDF in the name. Then use Procmon or Dependency Walker on the dll, it may suggest what's missing.Try simplifying the report, no database connection and just a "Hello World" in a text box and see if that works, then take the report having problems and start removing parts and see if it works at any point.

You really need to update your app, there are ways to improve performance in .NET SDK, load a dummy report on when your app opens, that preloads all of the CR runtime so when you open a real reports it should right away.

You never mentioned at what point the customer complained about the speed in your new app?

Good luck

entry.title: Re: Benefits of Executing Stored Procedures via API in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content: Looks Good Yoga..!

entry.title: Re: My app can not export PDF anymore after Windows 11 up...
entry.content: Don's suggestion to switch to 11.5 (at a minimum) may well solve the issue for you. I have an 11.5 version working at many customer sites, and I haven't encountered this issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud v2 Mashup with SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content: This is awesome yoga.. Looking forward to get more such C4C- Commissions Mashups. It will be really useful to customers.

entry.title: Re: How to Test Extensaibility in TDD System?
entry.content:
Hi Tomasz,

In your TDD, you essentially have two clients: 100 for Customizing and 80 for Development, along with CBC. (You don't have a 3-system landscape that includes a 100 Test or 100 Production system).

Both clients will have client-dependent configuration in CBC, meaning that configuration needs to be performed for each client independently (100 Customizing and 80 Development).

Key user extensibility is carried out in client 100, but the settings are client-independent.

Developer extensibility (using embedded ABAP - ADT in Eclipse) can only be done in client 80, and it is also client-independent.

Here's my recommendation for testing:

A) For key user extensibility (custom fields, BAdIs, etc.), you can create and test these in Customizing 100. B) For developer extensibility, you can only perform unit tests in client 80 (using the required configuration, which needs to be done in client 80 as the configuration is client-dependent).

However, if you need to test more complex scenarios, you can also observe the effects in client 100. Since the TDD configuration does not include a 100 test client, you'll need to decide what is needed and where to test. If the extensibility requires comprehensive testing, use client 100. However, if you only need to test some aspects of development (using ADT), use client 80 for testing the development.

I hope this makes sense.

entry.title: #UA WebinarSeries2025# Technology for Sustainability and ESG – ESG Management Systems and Artificial
entry.content:
Technology for Sustainability and ESG – ESG Management Systems and Artificial Intelligence

We live in a world where sustainability and corporate governance (ESG) have become a priority for companies and institutions across all sectors. But how can technology drive this revolution?

If you want to understand how data management and artificial intelligence are shaping the future of sustainability, don’t miss our Webinar: Technology for Sustainability and ESG – ESG Management Systems and Artificial Intelligence, happening on February 20, 2025.

This webinar will be led by Renata Amorim, SAP Sustainability Principal for Latin America, alongside industry experts who will explore real-world cases and discuss how technological innovations are helping businesses achieve their environmental and social goals.

What will you learn?

The event will be divided into two key sessions:

1. Mini Class: Fundamentals of Technology for Sustainability
In this session, you will gain clear insights into:

  • The urgency of sustainability and its impact across industries.
  • How technology is being applied to solve environmental and social challenges.
  • The importance of data management for sustainability.
  • Practical cases of technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain (Green Token), and ESG Management Systems.

    2. Interview with Pedro Rivas (ESPM)
    In the second part, we will welcome Professor Pedro Rivas, an expert in technology and sustainability, who will share valuable insights on:

    • The role of technology in addressing environmental challenges.
    • The growing impact of ESG indicators in financial markets.
    • How stricter regulations are driving the adoption of technological solutions.
    • The tech trends that will transform sustainability in the coming years.

      Why participate?

      • Stay up to date on technology applied to sustainability.
      • Gain exclusive insights from a renowned expert.
      • Discover innovative technologies that can make your company more efficient and ESG-aligned.
      • Engage in a live discussion and get your questions answered by experts.

        Don’t miss this opportunity to explore the future of sustainability through technology. Register now and be part of this essential discussion for the future of our planet!

entry.title: How to access SAP Build work zone URL from the SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud URL?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

How to access SAP Build work zone URL from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to access SAP Build work zone URL from the SAP S/...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

You can check the following documentations:

  • SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Expose Launchpad Content to Launchpads on SAP BTP
  • Integrating With SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
  • Products with Integration Capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

For more information, you can also check the KBA 3337753

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: EML: Read All Instances of a BO
entry.content:
In an ABAP RAP project, I want to read all instances of a BusinessObject via the EML statement "READ ..." like

READ ENTITIES OF zstore IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY store
ALL FIELDS
WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
RESULT result_stores.

but without providing any key, since I want to read all instances.

So, basically, I want to do a query "SELECT * FROM mytable" but as an EML statement for a BusinessObject.

Is that possible, and if so, how?

entry.title: Re: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)
entry.content:
As long as both output types are assigned to that access sequence and the output conditions are maintained appropriately, then you should see outputs generated. You'll want to check the determination analysis to make sure the proper condition record is found, and also review the dispatch time set on the output condition record - i.e. if the dispatch time is not set to 4 it will only be proposed, but not processed until further steps are taken (background job or other processing).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now - Generate a glossary compound document
entry.content: Thanks Shane, that worked a treat 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP SLT: How to match table /1LT/20000026666 to an ex...
entry.content:
Try this:

IUUC_LOGTAB_ID - Tables with /1CADMC/ in SM21

IUUC_TAB_ID - with /1LT/ in SM21

entry.title: Re: How to save/export business rules to PDF - truncates...
entry.content:
I was hoping someone would have a better option, but here is what I do.

I use SnagIt (or any screen grab tool should work) to copy and paste into a Word document. It does take a bit of work to make sure that everything is displayed. I use the Zoom on my browser and sometimes making it full screen makes it easier to capture.

This can get even more tedious if the rule is long, but it is the only way I have found to capture the rules.

If you want it in PDF form, you can Save As from the Word document and select the pdf as the file type.

I know this is not the answer you were probably hoping for, but just wanted to share my method.

Thanks,

Cindy

entry.title: Rows are getting duplicated due to 1 column
entry.content:
I have two columns named Id and approvers

1st row Id no 1 has values David;Musta;

2 nd row Id no 1 has values Vinay;

3rd row Id no 1 has values Kronid;

I want the outcome as single line and Id should have only 1 and Approvers values should be David;Musta;Vinay;Kronid 

can someone help me on this please

entry.title: Re: SAP SLT: How to match table /1LT/20000026666 to an ex...
entry.content: IUUC_LOGTAB_ID and IUUC_TAB_ID are table names

entry.title: Re: In Shipper Scenario ,How we can Explode Multiple Stages in Freight Unit from Sales order /Delive
entry.content: can anybody provide configuration set up in details

entry.title: Re: MRP Exception Messages list - introductory information
entry.content: Very Good information

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content:
Thank you for this article.✔️

 

Very well done.😊

entry.title: Re: Some employees are not being synced to IAS/IPS from S...
entry.content: Apologies for the delay! According to SAP Support, the issue with some users not syncing to IAS was due to the extension status not being set to 0 or 1. You can find the extension status by navigating to Data Inspector -> Table_USER_ACCOUNT and filter on extension status=2. SAP must run a script on their end to resolve. I hope this helps if you are still encountering an issue.

entry.title: FIFO - valuation based on Valuation Type
entry.content:
Hi,

I activated FIFO at Company Level.

My Customer has some materials managed at Split valuation.
We have 2 split valuation: The first "VAL_COM" is valorized at standard price. The second "NN_VAL_COM" is always valorized at zero, because the stock are asset-

When I run the Tcode MRF1 the system calculate the total stock summed both of split valuation "NN_VAL_COM" and "VAL_COM". In this way the final result of FIFO is wrong.

 

 

I have seen that in the table MYMFT there is the field "Valuation Type"(BWTAR) but in my case is always blank.

 

Is there some method to exclude the stock in split valuation "NN_VAL_COM" of the FIFO Calculate or manage the calculation of FIFO split in Valuation type?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)
entry.content: I understand,  the dispatch time is set to 4. Output type A has AC set to 0001, while for output type B, I set it to 0002. Now, in the Access Sequences for 0002, after selecting it and going to the overview, the standard entry with table 11 (Sales org/billing type) is displayed, the requirement field is empty, and exclusive is checked. After going to Fields, if I enter the designation of the Polish sales department in the VKORG (Sales org) field in the Spec. Val. Source, will this be correct and should everything work properly? For the Billing Type, the condition should apply to all invoices.

entry.title: Re: Error:Ship Notice line number reference is not allowe...
entry.content: no

entry.title: Re: Error:Ship Notice line number reference is not allowe...
entry.content: Raise SR for enabling the toggle CSC-16955

entry.title: Re: Partner functions in Customer master
entry.content: Create a new Partner function ZABC with copy from Account Group 0007

entry.title: Re: Adopting SuccessFactors Talent intelligence hub: Where to Begin?
entry.content: @amydobson  , It should be ready next week. Please watch this space. I will update in this blog.

entry.title: Re: Conditions for Output - billing (NACE)
entry.content: @zakk if you are using Salesorg/billing type then you would need to create condition records for every billing type in use. A different option would be a custom access sequence or identifying one that only uses sales organization (not sure if there is one off the top of my head).

entry.title: Open Items Display in FBL1N but F-44 is not taking the Open Itesm to be cleared
entry.content:
Hi:

When I ran the FBL1N the open Items are showed but, when I try to clear them on F-44; system message indicates that there is no open item in the Vendor Subledger Account.

Message F5170

Do you know why this weird case is?

Do you know if there is something wrong with one table? If so, what table could be?

entry.title: Ship to address sales report
entry.content:
Hi! Is there a way I can get sales report that includes ship to address/ or how I can cobble together multiple reports? This is for state sales apportionment. 

thank you!

entry.title: CAP where clause to filter dates in an As Projection Variant entity service
entry.content:
Hello community. 

I'm currently trying to use a where clause to filter by dates (only to show a list of items created in a 30 days range from the current day), in my entity service created using the As Projection Varian approach. The problem is that I'm not sure how to have that value date dynamically calculated into a sort of variable, so the filter can occur.

The OData service is being consumed by an 3rd party app, in which the user can post items in the table. But also the external app is getting the list of items created, but they need to be filtered using the criteria described above (today - 30days).

Any insight would be highly appreciated.

The model:

The entity service exposed with the where clause incomplete

entry.title: Re: FTP CPI Connectivity failed: SOCKS5 command failed wi...
entry.content:
Hello, 

I had the same issue and resolved it as follow (I am aware that you already confirmed passive mode is enabled)

1.- Checking if passive mode is enabled 

2.- Getting the port range for passive mode. This is mandatory without this info it won't work, so your FTP admin should be able to provide that info. For Linux, there is a "ftpd" conf file in /etc where you can check the range, My scenario was with Linux. 

3.- In cloud connector you need two entries: 

            a) One entry with the port range of passive mode 

            b) additional entry for port 21 (normal FTP port) --> This is the control connection 

That should do it, it worked for me. 

Good luck. 

Kind regards!

entry.title: SAP B1 Create Purchase Order from Sales Blanket agreement
entry.content:
Hi There,

We have Sales Blanket agreements with customers and we always purchase all items from Overseas vendors for blanket agreement its. In the past we used to create Sales Order and create Purchase Order from it. But sales order does not work exactly like Blanket Agreements for customers who have blankets.

We do not create Purchasing Blanket agreement with Vendors. They produce everything as per order and ship directly to my warehouse. 

How do I create Purchase Orders from Blanket agreement and have relationship map showing the relation?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Including a curriculum in a program?
entry.content: Hi! How do I configure a Program to recognize prior completions of individual Courses in that Program? If someone already previously completed a course in that Program, I don't want them to need to do it again.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content:
Hi @Daggolu_PremSai,

Thank you for your comment !

I updated the github repository with the searchCodeTool.zip, you can directly download it and upload it in BTP. Please let me know if you have any further issues.

Thank you, 

Fatima

entry.title: Re: Does the SAP Enable Now Certification will be retired...
entry.content: Hi @Margit_Wagner so this means that on April 2026 this certification will dissapear from any certified person correct?

entry.title: SAP ASE 16.0 redhat 9 raw device
entry.content:
Bonjour everyone,

   I'd like to know if anyone managed to create raw devices on redhat 9 to provide to SAP ASE Database?

Starting in Redhat 9, the binaries for /bin/raw are not shiped anymore and a different method should be use to manage raw devices.

Also, are there still advantages going with raw devices instead of filesystem, I know it useds to but with today's technology, I don't know anymore!?

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Automation for SAP Business ByDesign: Bots Available
entry.content:
Hello,  Thanks for this summary of the IRPA functionality, it now has some areas I was not aware of yet. The links under Useful KBA's are not valid.  Also, is this a good place to bookmark as SAP improves the AI functionality of BYD?   Thanks! 

Carol Casey

entry.title: day wise Cleared documents fetching
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would to fetch Vendor cleared documents on Daily basis in R/3 system. say for example if 100 documents are cleared on today then tomorrow I am trying to fetch all the 100 cleared documents based on Clearing documents.

for the purpose i had written logic to fetch Clearing documents first from BKPF based on CPUDT field then passing to BSAK as below.

Now I am facing Performance issue because in BSAK table  AUGGJ and BLART are not key fields and there are no secondary indexes also available. could any one help me to improve the performance ar finding alternative way?

Thank you in Advance.

entry.title: Optimize Your Company Profile for Better Lead Matching
entry.content:
All B2B sales organizations know how crucial lead generation is to the success of their business. As critical as it is, 68% of B2B businesses report that they struggle with lead generation. Clearly, lead generation, and in particular, high quality lead generation, is a challenge in the B2B space. SAP Business Network offers tools for suppliers to generate leads for their business from thousands of the world’s largest companies looking for products and services. With over six trillion dollars transacted annually, the network is an ideal space for any B2B supplier to grow their business. 

Optimizing your profile for better leads 

As buyers search for products and services on SAP Business Network, information from supplier company profiles, in addition to network catalog content, is used to populate the search results. By having a complete, and up to date profile, you can optimize your chances of generating leads. 

In particular, the following criteria from company profiles is used in buyer searches: 

  • Company name- Identify who you are to buyers 
  • Certificates- Build trust and credibility by demonstrating your commitment to quality standards, expertise in your field, adherence to industry best practices, etc. 
  • Keywords- Increase searchability when a buyer search matches your keywords 
  • Ship to/service locations- Show buyers if they are in your service range 
  • Product and service categories- Help potential customers understand your offerings and increase your searchability 
Check out this helpful checklist to make sure you fully complete your profile and increase your chances of receiving leads from interested buyers. 

Generating leads with a network catalog 

The profile and SAP Business Network catalog work in concert for suppliers to get discovered by buyers and generate leads. With the network catalog, organizations can upload a master catalog of all their products and services for all network buyers to search. As interested buyers send requests for information (RFIs) from catalog items, hot leads are generated for the supplier. Each network catalog SKU has a link back to the supplier’s company profile where interested buyers can learn more about the seller. Read this blog to learn more about network catalogs.  

Maximize your potential for leads 

Launching in February 2025, the SAP Business Network promote subscription will offer tools designed to fully maximize your potential for leads as a network supplier, such as unlimited network catalog publishing edits, open APIs, GenAI capabilities, profile verification, insights and more. It will be available for purchase in the portal. Visit this page to learn more about promote. 

Complete Your Profile Today 

If you don’t already have an account, you can register for a free SAP Business Network, standard account in just a few minutes, or log in if you already have an account to get started. 

Register or login to complete your profile.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP ByDesign's OData API to update WorkplaceAdd...
entry.content: Hi Anand,

entry.title: Re: Using SAP ByDesign's OData API to update WorkplaceAdd...

However, I am not able to find any documentation on this in the ByD SOAP docs (https://api.sap.com/package/BYD/soap). Can you help me find some documentation?

entry.title: Re: Creating a Contact Person from Supplier CR UI
entry.content: just got the answer. it is not possible when we create CP from supplier UI.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Rollout - Americas
entry.content:
Our regional GTM team objective is to enable internal stakeholders across SAP and drive adoption of SAP Cloud ALM among customers in NA & LAC. We reach customers across the market units through in person events like Roadshows and Deep dive sessions delivered in strategic locations across Americas. And we conduct webinars to scale the customer reach through Awareness sessions and SAP Solution specific deep dive webinars delivering rich content to enable and empower customers on their SAP ALM strategy and Cloud ALM adoption journey. We do try and engage one on one with customers to provide strategic guidance and build reference success stories across the market units in Americas

SAP Cloud ALM is the significant core of the integrated tool chain to support RISE with SAP methodology integrating with SAP Signavio, LeanIX and Tricentis. SAP Cloud ALM largely supports the various phases of SAP Customer Value Journey

OUR TEAM: 

email: sapalmus@sap.com
NA TEAM:

Vijay Prakash

Umesh Jagdesh

Rony Muthiraparampil  

Nandhini Ravisankar

Prathap Gunasekaran 

Ankita Nandanwar

LA TEAM:

Willian Heissler

Luiz Almeida

Vitor Wolke

Fernando Rocha 

Laura Leote

entry.title: WBS Element replication API
entry.content:
We would like to know which API could be used to Replicate WBS Elements from S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: WBS Element replication API
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


You can make use of API WBS Element - Replicate Data to replicate WBS data from one system to another. Kindly go through the documentation below:

WBS Element - Replicate Data | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

WBS Element - Replicate Data | SAP Help Portal


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow NA 2025 - Palo Alto and Dallas!
entry.content:
Join Us for the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow NA 2025!

After our successful events in Latin America, we are excited to announce our next stop: Palo Alto, California (March 4th 2025) followed by Dallas, Texas(March 6th 2025)! Mark your calendar, for an exclusive opportunity to immerse yourself in the latest advancements in SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (Cloud ALM).

Why Attend?

This event is packed with valuable insights, new capabilities, and unique networking opportunities, making it a must-attend for anyone interested in optimizing their cloud journey with SAP Cloud ALM.

Discover SAP Cloud ALM
SAP Cloud ALM is a comprehensive suite of cloud-based tools designed to support every aspect of your application lifecycle— from development and deployment to monitoring and continuous optimization. Enhance efficiency, minimize risks, and ensure the success of your projects through effective team collaboration.

Key Benefits for Attendees

  • Exclusive Insights: Gain in-depth knowledge of the latest SAP Cloud ALM tools and strategies.
  • Networking Opportunities: Connect with SAP experts, industry leaders, and peers to share experiences and build professional relationships.

Featured Topics

  • RISE with SAP and Clean Core Support Strategies: Explore how SAP Cloud ALM integrates with RISE with SAP for seamless migrations and optimizations.
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise Strategies: Leverage SAP Cloud ALM to align your SAP systems with future-proof digital transformation strategies.
  • Automating Testing with Tricentis: Accelerate project cycles and enhance quality assurance through advanced testing automation.
  • Optimizing Operations with AI: Utilize AI-powered solutions within SAP Cloud ALM to boost operational efficiency and decision-making.
  • Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager: Receive a step-by-step guide for a seamless migration from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM.

Event Details

Date: March 4, 2025
Location: Pebble Beach, PAL01
3410 Hillview Avenue
Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
Time: Half-day event, including networking and consultation opportunities

 

Date: March 6, 2025
Location: SAP America, Dallas
7500 Windrose Avenue (Suite 250)
Plano, TX 75024
Time: Half-day event, including networking and consultation opportunities

Register Now!

Seats are limited! Secure your spot today for the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow NA 2025.

Register Here

We look forward to welcoming you at the Roadshows, and showcasing how SAP Cloud ALM can revolutionize your business.

For more information and updates, stay connected with us!

Cyril

entry.title: Subaccount certificate renewal in SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

I have a concern regarding the Subaccount certificate renewal, and it will expire about 20days from today. 

The problem is this subaccount certificate is not under HEC and the Initiated By user SXXXXX is no longer available.

Is it possible to user my own S-User ID to refresh this subaccount certificate?

Please advise Thank you.

entry.title: Re: BTM TMS Transports - SLP_CTS: Error during client cre...
entry.content: Hi Fernando i had the same error, after some time i realized i was sent an email by sap alerting from faild attempts to log on when i started import selected in Transport Manager. I updated my account password and changed in target system destination and it worked, i hope it helps you

entry.title: Re: Sales Register
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if you have further questions

entry.title: FV50 restrict being able to post to cost center or network activity based on GL account
entry.content:
We need to be able to restrict journal entries (FV50) based on G/L accounts, basically using the following logic.

  • If a user is posting to a 6XXXXX G/L account they should only be able to post to a cost center
  • If a user is posting to a 5XXXXX G/L account they should only be able to post to a network activity

I have seen some mention of validation rules via user exists that might be the answer.  Can anyone confirm that or help point in the correct direction?

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content: Eclipse 2024-12 is not yet approved with HANA tools, try to use 2024-09

entry.title: Common Configuration Issues When Using Git in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Introduction

Although Git may seem like a trivial feature in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), it plays a critical role in project development. It is an essential tool for collaborating with your development team. A typical developer’s day often starts with a git pull to stay up to date and ends with a git push to share their progress.

However, encountering unexpected errors during these operations can be frustrating. Interestingly, Git-related issues generate a disproportionately high number of customer support tickets in BAS compared to other components.

According to the SAP BAS document on Connecting to a Corporate Git Repository, when using an on-premise Git backend, Cloud Connector (SCC) and a properly configured subaccount destination are required to allow BAS, a cloud application, to access it. In this article, I will highlight some common Git connection issues caused by incorrect destination and SCC configurations and how to resolve them.

1. Incorrect Destination Properties

When configuring the destination, ensure that the following properties are correctly set:

  • WebIDEEnabled = true
  • HTML5.DynamicDestination = true

Failing to configure these properties correctly can cause BAS to fail when connecting to your on-premise Git system, often resulting in 500 or 502 errors depending on whether http or https is used in the Git command.

  • If your destination URL uses HTTP (http://), you may encounter: 

 

 

 

  • If your destination URL uses HTTPS (https://), you might see: 

Note The ProxyType of an On-Premise destination is HTTP, but the communication between BAS cloud application and SCC is encrypted and safe, there's no much difference whether the destination URL and the Git command uses "http://" or "https://". 

Besides above two properties, misuse of other properties can cause failures too. In one support case, a customer said they correctly set up Cloud Connector and the subaccount destination but still encountered a 500 Internal Server Error. Even with detailed logging enabled, git clone provided no additional clues beyond the error message. Git requests never reached the Cloud Connector at all. After extensive troubleshooting together with various teams, we discovered that the customer had mistakenly set the HTML5.ForwardAuthToken property to true. This property is designed to send oAuth token to the destination, it should not be turned on for an on-premise type destination or a destination with NoAuthentication because there's no oAuth token to forward at all.

The customer wasn’t sure why or when they added this property, but simply removing it resolved the issue.

2. Letter case mismatch in hostname

To expose an on-premise Git system, you must map it to a virtual host (hostname + port) in SCC. This virtual host is used when defining the destination URL and in Git commands.

While hostnames are generally case-insensitive, different behaviors across SCC, BTP Cockpit, and BAS can cause unexpected errors.

SCC: Uppercase letters are not allowed in both virtual and internal hostnames, the Administration Console converts them to lowercase.

BTP Cockpit: Allows uppercase letters in the hostname for the destination URL, the check connection test may still succeed if there're uppercase letters in the URL.

SAP BAS: Treats hostnames in the destination URL as case-insensitive.

For example, if your destination URL is "https://mygit:443", BAS will successfully match it, regardless of how you type it in Git commands:

  • git clone https://MyGit:443 
  • git clone https://myGit:443 
  • git clone https://MYGIT:443 

However, SCC (Expose Intranet Systems) requires that the destination URL hostname exactly matches the virtual hostname defined in Cloud Connector. A mismatch can result in 500 or 502 errors.

Recommendation  

To avoid inconsistencies, always use lowercase hostnames when configuring SCC mappings, destination URLs, and Git commands in BAS. This reduces the risk of unexpected errors caused by inconsistent case handling across different systems.

3. Duplicate destinations with the same hostname

When executing a Git command, BAS generally selects a destination whose URL matches both the hostname and port number in the Git command. If there's no port number specified, it defaults to 443 for https and 80 for http.

In BTP, you can create multiple destinations with the same hostname but different port numbers or properties. However, this can lead to unexpected behavior when using Git in BAS. It may unpredictably routing your Git command through an unintended destination to a wrong backend system.

Example Scenario

Destinations Defined in BTP

NameURL
Dest1http://git.int.mycompany.com:8088
Dest2http://git.int.mycompany.com:44300
Dest3http://git.int.mycompany.com:443

Resource mappings in SCC

 Virtual HostInternal HostProtocol
Resource1git.int.mycompany.com:8088localhost:3001https
Resource2git.int.mycompany.com:44300localhost:3002https
Resource3git.int.mycompany.com:443localhost:443https

When running Git commands in my BAS: 

BAS always used the third destination (Dest3), routing requests to localhost:443, regardless of the specified different port. However, after deleting Dest3, the first two Git commands correctly reached their intended destinations (localhost:3001 and localhost:3002). A little wierd!😵? 

Recommendation

To avoid confusion caused by above behavior:

  • Create only one destination per virtual host.
  • Avoid relying on port numbers for differentiation (even though SCC supports it).
  • When adding a new resource mapping in SCC, use a new virtual hostname instead of different ports.

4. Host In Request Header in SCC

In SCC, there's a hostInHeader property in resource mapping, corresponding to the "Host In Request Header" field in SCC Admin Console.

This property controls the value of the Host http request header before SCC forwardsthe request to the backend.  If hostInHeader is virtual, the Host header will be the virtual host (same as what you use in BAS git commands, no modification); otherwise SCC modifies the Host header to the internal host.

Although this setting typically doesn’t affect most web servers, some systems impose strict security policies requiring that the Host header matches their website hostname. In such cases, setting hostInHeader = internal is necessary to prevent 503 Service Unavailable or other errors.

Conclusion

By following recommendations in this article, understanding and properly configuring destination and SCC, removing duplicate destinations, you can avoid common Git-related issues and have a pleasant experience working with Git in SAP BAS.

entry.title: API_PROJECT_V3(Create multiple WBS nodes and Get Next Available Project Number)
entry.content:
Hello community. 

I'm trying to create a PS Project(CJ20N) using the API_PROJECT_V3 from Integration Suite and I'm having two problems which I'm not sure what's the best way to solve. 

When I'm going to create the Project, the sending system gives me an existing Project Number so I can use as template, with this Number I also should get the next available number and create it, on S4 I can use the FM CN_SUCHE_FREIE_NUMMER to do that, but it's not an RFC and I didn't find any API to get that number. Is there any way I didn't find or should I create a wrapper for this FM?

My other problem is that I'm trying to copy the WBS structure from the template project into the new one. For that I first read the old Project and then use its data on the POST function. But on the Help Portal I don't find any field to make the link between children and parent WBS nodes. I think I can use WBSElementParentInternalID even though it's not documented on the CREATE structure, but I'm not sure how to fill it since when creating I still don't have the Internal ID

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Rows are getting duplicated due to 1 column
entry.content:
Define the following variables:

v_approvers:  =(Previous(Self; ([Id]))+([approvers]))

v_approvers_max:  =NoFilter(Max([v_approvers])) ForEach ([Id])

v_status:   =NoFilter(If ([v_approvers] = Max([v_approvers]) In ([Id])) Then "D"; All )

then hide the coulmn "approvers" and apply the filter (v_status equal to "D") to the table

...

hope it helps

entry.title: Subject Name Identifiier in IAS
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a question regarding the Subject Name Identifier in IAS.

I’ve been experimenting in my test environment and here's what I did:

  • In Azure, I created a Claim employee_id=user.employee_id.
  • In IAS, I set the Subject Name Identifier to Corporate Identity Provider=employee_id.
  • The user was created in SAC/BTP with the User Name being the employee_id (User Name filed = 131414)

Then I changed the employee_id in Azure, but the user was still able to log in.

Only when I removed the email=user.email claim in Azure (ODIC), the user could no longer log in to BTP.

I thought the SNI was the main factor for identifying the user in the cloud app.

So, for example, if the user with SNI=234324 is sent to BTP, and only the user with SNI (User Name in Cloud App) 1111 exists in BTP, the user should no longer be able to log in – but this is not the case. It seems that, even though the setting in IAS - Subject Name Identifier=employee_id , the user is identified/found by the email address in BTP

Why is that? Or did I misunderstand the purpose of the Subject Name Identifier?

Do I understand it correctly that the Subject Name Identifier is always equal to the User Name in a cloud app (SNI = User Name in SAC)

Thank you very much.

Best Regards
